_doc: Template: pure teleop recording. Use this when an operator drives the robot through the leader / spacemouse / phone and frames are saved to disk. Underscore-prefixed keys (_doc, _comment_*) are inline annotations stripped by the validator — leave them in while you fill the rest, or delete them once you've memorised the schema.
_comment_schema_version: Always '1' for this MR. Bump when the schema changes incompatibly.

robot._comment_type: Options: 'arxx5_bimanual', 'bi_piper_follower', 'so100_follower', 'so101_follower', 'koch_follower', 'piper'. Must match a key registered in src/lerobot/robots/.
robot._comment_id: Free-form id string. ARX convention: stringified physical ID, e.g. '2', '5', '8'.

hardware_meta._comment_cameras: Camera names are arbitrary; downstream models care about the set head/left_wrist/right_wrist (+ optionally low_head, third_view). index_or_path: integer → /dev/videoN (run `lerobot-find-cameras opencv` to find the right number), string → arbitrary path. Typical resolution 640x480, fps 30; bump to 1280x720 for low_head if you need close-up detail.

end_effector._comment_end_effector: Per-arm end-effector configuration. Each side carries {type, model}. type options: 'gripper' (future: suction, soft hand). model options: 'long', 'short' — affects gripper open/close thresholds; pick what's physically installed. Bimanual rigs may run mismatched grippers (e.g. left=long, right=short); the schema makes that explicit.

collection_meta._comment_operator_name: Human running this run (pinyin or username). Lands in dataset metadata and batch repo_id.
collection_meta._comment_adversary_operator: Second human; only used in self_play mode where two humans alternate. Leave "" for record/infer/infer_record.
collection_meta._comment_is_adversary: true only in self_play mode (mirrors having a destroyer role).
collection_meta._comment_acting_arms: Which arm(s) are actively driven during this session. Options: ['left', 'right'] (default, bi-manual), ['left'], or ['right']. Lets downstream training filter / weight single-arm vs bi-manual data without grepping prompts.
collection_meta._comment_mode: Authoritative mode selector. Must be 'record' here. Other templates: infer / infer_record / self_play.

task_meta._comment_task_name: Lowercase snake_case identifier, e.g. 'pack_socks', 'string_bead', 'seatbelt'. Used in repo_id and as the dataset prefix.

task_stage._comment_mode: 'full' for end-to-end episodes. Custom values flag a sub-task variant (e.g. recovery-only data).
task_stage._comment_stages: Optional freeform sub-task tag, e.g. 'single.recover_2_left_move'. Surfaces in dataset metadata and helps disambiguate variants for downstream filtering.

task_meta._comment_objects: Free-form {name: {attr: value}} dict. Values are usable in prompts via {name.attr} (e.g. set objects.socks.color='white' then write 'Pack the {socks.color} socks.').

task_description: FILL_ME_IN — describe the scene + acceptance criteria so collectors know what 'success' looks like.
_comment_task_description: Natural-language description shown in the preflight popup. Chinese is fine — preflight uses a CJK-capable font. Operators glance at this to confirm they're running the right task.

task_spec._comment_task_spec: Either an inline dict (this template) or a path string pointing to a JSON file. Inline is preferred — one artifact per session.

operator.prompt: FILL_ME_IN. Optionally use {placeholders} from task_meta.objects (e.g. {socks.color}) or collection_meta (e.g. {operator}, {city}, {site}).
operator._comment_prompt: Language instruction sent to the policy. Supports {a.b.c} template substitution from task_meta.objects + collection_meta. {speed} survives substitution and is filled per-episode by RoleSpec.get_prompt_for_episode if speed_variation is set.
operator._comment_max_time_s: Per-role timeout. null in record mode (cfg.dataset.episode_time_s is authoritative). Used in self_play where each role has its own clock.

recording._comment_num_episodes: How many episodes to record before the session ends. Operator can Ctrl+Esc early.
recording._comment_episode_time_s: Per-episode timeout in seconds. Hard stop even if no success/failure key pressed.
recording._comment_reset_time_s: Sleep between episodes so the operator can reset the scene. 0 = no pause.
recording._comment_fps: Control loop and dataset write rate. Most rigs are 30; some bipiper setups go up to 60.
recording._comment_video: true → encode camera streams to mp4 via NVENC (or libx264 fallback). false → keep raw frames (huge).
recording._comment_display_data: Open the rerun.io viewer alongside the recorder so the operator sees what the cameras see.
recording._comment_play_sounds: Audio cues (start of episode, success, failure). Falls back to OS TTS if HTTP TTS server unreachable.
recording._comment_enable_log_say: If false, the recorder still logs text but skips audio. Use for noisy lab environments or unattended runs.
recording._comment_enable_logging: Set false to suppress the AsyncLogger entirely. Almost always leave true.
recording._comment_progress_print_interval_s: Per-tick progress line throttle. 1.0 = once per second. 0 = print every tick (~30 Hz, noisy).
recording._comment_auto_success: true skips the y/n end-of-episode confirmation. Use for fully-autonomous infer_record runs where you trust the policy's stop signal.
recording._comment_resume: true → continue writing to an existing dataset directory. false → expect to start fresh (errors if the dir exists).
recording._comment_debug: Verbose logging. Operators leave false; flip to true when diagnosing.
recording._comment_data_root: Where datasets land on disk. $HOME / $VAR are expanded at write time. Override with --dataset.root=... on the CLI for one-off runs.

_comment_inference: Empty in record mode (no policy runs). See templates/infer*.json / self_play.json for the full set of inference knobs.
_comment_intervention: Empty in record mode (no policy → no intervention loop). See self_play.json for the available knobs.
_comment_self_play: Empty in record mode. See self_play.json.
